title: angelica archangelica subsp. archangelica
intro: Angelica archangelica subsp. archangelica, also known as European angelica or wild celery, is a biennial plant of the carrot family (Apiaceae) that is native to Europe, western Asia, and the mountains of North Africa. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, which are attributed to its high content of essential oils, flavonoids, and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Angelica archangelica subsp. archangelica include its ability to relieve digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies for colds, coughs, and other illnesses. Herbal preparations of Angelica archangelica subsp. archangelica include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to soothe the body and promote overall well-being.
